assertions.reason: A Node smoke script passed a stub vault into EnboxUserAgent.create() and confirmed the resulting agent reused that exact object as agent.vault.
The same runtime smoke script showed typeof agent.vault.initialize === 'function' and agent.initialize({ password: 'x' }) forwarded to the stub initialize method and returned 'rp'.
Two isolated Node smoke checks confirmed both the no-arg create() path and the explicit agentVault: undefined path produced an HdIdentityVault instance.
bun run typecheck completed successfully with the committed positive contract fixture src/__contract__/vault-injection.ts in scope.
A temporary incompatible agentVault fixture caused tsc --noEmit to fail with TS2740 referencing IdentityVault, proving the widened type did not collapse to any.
The clean typecheck run reported auth_error_matches=0, so the widened vault typing produced no diagnostics under node_modules/@enbox/auth.
After bun install --frozen-lockfile revalidated the patched dependency tree, bun run verify exited 0 with lint, typecheck, and all Jest suites passing.

frictions.description: Running both VAL-PATCH-006 default-path checks back-to-back against the default DATA/AGENT vault store in one Node process did not complete cleanly after the first HdIdentityVault opened the store.
frictions.resolution: Validated the no-arg and explicit-undefined cases in isolated Node processes and used a separate dataPath for the explicit-undefined case.

summary: All 7 assigned assertions passed on the local jest-local surface. Runtime smoke checks, positive and negative typecheck validations, and the aggregate verify command all succeeded; bun run verify finished with 10/10 suites and 56/56 tests passing.
